pi_items.source: Verified through SOC 17-2112 definitions showing Industrial Engineers work in manufacturing with PLM responsibilities

pi_items.verified_value: Industrial Engineer (SOC 17-2112); Cost Engineer is not a formal SOC code but reasonable descriptor
pi_items.source: SOC code 17-2112 is officially 'Industrial Engineers'. Cost analysis is listed as part of Industrial Engineer responsibilities. Cost Estimators exist as SOC 13-1051.
Confirmed via O*NET, BLS, and multiple occupational classification sources
BLS and O*NET official documentation show the major group code 17-0000 is titled 'Architecture and Engineering Occupations', not 'Architecture and Engineering'

pi_items.metadata_value: Azure Data Studio + Microsoft SQL Server 2022
pi_items.verified_value: Azure Data Studio + Microsoft SQL Server 2022
pi_items.source: Microsoft Learn documentation confirms Azure Data Studio is compatible with SQL Server 2022. Note: Azure Data Studio was retired February 28, 2026.
Official Microsoft SQL Server sample database available at https://github.com/Microsoft/sql-server-samples/releases/download/adventureworks/AdventureWorks2022.bak

pi_items.metadata_value: AdventureWorks2022 Production schema (Microsoft sample database with multi-level product BOM data)
pi_items.verified_value: AdventureWorks2022 Production schema contains multi-level BOM data for bicycles with actual component hierarchies
pi_items.source: Microsoft Learn documentation and Dataedo schema documentation confirm AdventureWorks2022 contains Production.BillOfMaterials with multi-level bicycle assembly data
Confirmed via Dataedo AdventureWorks documentation; all three tables exist in Production schema

pi_items.verified_value: 7 columns: AssemblyProductID, AssemblyName, ComponentID, ComponentName, BOMLevel, PerAssemblyQty, UnitMeasureCode
pi_items.source: Task README lines 71-81 and export_result.sh lines 34-42 confirm exactly 7 required columns for the view

pi_items.verified_value: 6-7 columns: AssemblyProductID, AssemblyName, TotalBOMComponents, MaxBOMDepth, DirectMaterialCost, TotalMaterialCost (plus optional ReportTimestamp)
pi_items.source: export_result.sh lines 81-90 explicitly checks for 6 columns (REQUIRED_TABLE_COLS array). Task description mentions timestamp. Verifier.py lines 165-167 confirms 6 primary columns.

pi_items.verified_value: dbo.usp_GenerateBOMCostReport must exist and populate LifecycleCostSummary
pi_items.source: Task description, setup_task.sh line 14-15, export_result.sh line 117-118, verifier.py line 236-242

pi_items.verified_value: Data is real (from Microsoft official AdventureWorks2022 sample database), not synthetic
pi_items.source: Setup script downloads from official Microsoft GitHub releases; AdventureWorks is a decades-old sample database, not generated for this task

privileged_info_summary: The task uses AdventureWorks2022, an official Microsoft SQL Server sample database containing real multi-level product bill-of-materials data for bicycles. The task requires creating a recursive CTE view with 7 specific columns to traverse the Production.BillOfMaterials hierarchy. The table metadata claim of 8 columns is contradicted by implementation which checks for 6 required columns (plus optional timestamp). The SOC major group is officially 'Architecture and Engineering Occupations' not 'Architecture and Engineering'. The underlying data is real, not synthetic. The occupation classification as Industrial Engineer (SOC 17-2112.00) under Manufacturing/PLM domain is verified; Cost Engineer is not a formal SOC code but represents valid responsibilities within the Industrial Engineer role.
